quotes.verbatim_quote: The art of accomplishing long reaches is based on relaxed power control—not a large hand.
quotes.why_load_bearing: States Van Eps's core thesis on extended fingerings: reach is a function of relaxed tendon control, not anatomy, which justifies prescribing wide-spread studies to all players.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Reach must be developed gradually by patiently working with material that contains extended spreads. It must be practiced on the
fingerboard under actual playing conditions—not forcing fingers apart with wedges etc.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Rejects mechanical stretching aids in favor of in-context practice, defining the only sanctioned route to the extended spreads the method demands.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Forcing the fingers by tensing tightens the tendons and actually shortens the reach.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Names the counter-productive failure mode (tensing) that students will instinctively use, framing relaxation as not just preferred but mechanically necessary.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
The G47°35 above is also an E diminished 9th-in other words the symbol for a chord depends on its
use-its place in a progression etc.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Establishes that Van Eps's chord labels are functional/contextual rather than absolute, which is essential for reading the substitution-heavy chromatic triad studies.

quotes.topic: Constructing all scales from chromatic exercises
quotes.verbatim_quote:
By using
to up

these chromatic exercises in all their various forms, positions, and combinations, all
scales Maj., Min., Dim., Aug. may be constructed.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Claims chromatic third-to-tenth exercises as a universal scale-construction engine, justifying the chapter's central drill as foundational rather than ornamental.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Construct your own exercises by using portions of these forms, and all the other exercises in these
books. use 1 note, 2 notes, 4 notes etc. Selected from the beginning, the middle or any place in the
groups. Practise of this nature helps to increase reach and flexibility. If muscular discomfort is
noticed, stop, rest the hand. Some discomfort is normal.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Authorizes the student to fragment Van Eps's written exercises into custom drills, embedding generative practice as part of the method.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Always Treat each measure as a sep@rate exercise-sustain the whole note for the full measure
quotes.why_load_bearing: Defines the per-measure practice unit and the sustain-the-pedal rule that together give every chromatic triad station its lap-piano character.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Notice that the sustained note is describing the C Major scale. All the
quotes.why_load_bearing: Identifies the outer-voice anchor as a melodic scale carrier under the moving chromatic inner line—Van Eps's outer-voice-anchor principle in action.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
When working with long chromatic lines while sustaining a tone, the fingers have to sound two,
sometimes three half steps in succession with the same finger, smoothly.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Identifies the unusual single-finger chromatic walk required to keep the pedal sustained, a signature left-hand demand of Van Eps's chromatic work.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
The Minor Modes Can't Parallel the Major because of the Minor third interval-it is shortened by one

semitone
quotes.why_load_bearing: Explains why minor-key chromatic-upper-voice studies require their own treatment rather than transposition of major-key forms, motivating the parallel minor exercises.
